title: cached_network_image
objectives: Cache network images on disk with placeholders

sections.markdown: `cached_network_image` downloads network images once and reuses them from disk/memory. Provides `placeholder` and `errorWidget` so lists don't flash blank or crash on bad URLs.
Set `memCacheWidth`/`memCacheHeight` to the widget's physical size. Otherwise a 4000-px hero image decodes at full resolution into an 80-px thumbnail — a common OOM cause in list screens.

Assuming the cache is unbounded/permanent. It's an LRU with defaults you can tune (`DefaultCacheManager`). Call `emptyCache()` on logout for privacy or `removeFile(url)` when an image is updated server-side.
Combine with a server-side resize (Firebase resize-images extension, imgproxy, or a CDN). Request the correct variant instead of one giant image for all screens.

flashcards.back: Decodes the image at the display size, avoiding OOM when a huge image ends up in a small widget.

flashcards.front: How to clear cache on logout?
flashcards.back: `DefaultCacheManager().emptyCache()` (or a custom cache manager for scoped clears).
Keeps list UI stable while loading, and handles broken URLs without blank frames.

quiz.q: The most impactful setting for memory is…
quiz.explain: Decoding at target size prevents huge bitmaps in small widgets.

quiz.q: To clear cached images on logout…

challenges.text: Show a network image with a shimmer placeholder + error icon.
Add memCacheWidth to a grid of thumbnails; measure memory before/after.
Force refetch when the server updates an image (bust cache).
Use a custom BaseCacheManager scoped to a feature; empty it on feature exit.

interviewQuestions: Why cache network images at all?
How to avoid OOMs from huge images?
How is the cache stored and evicted?
How to bust the cache when server updates?
